---

The below material is an abbreviated guide to running Lorcana tournaments in the melee.gg software. Full, and more thorough, documentation on melee.gg can be found on their documentation page [here](https://melee.gg/Documentation)

# Lorcana Event Scheduling
>To create a Lorcana event you will need to log into the [Melee Tournament System](https://melee.gg) using credentials provided by your password manager. Once there you can navigate to the [Your Tournaments](https://melee.gg/Profile/Tournaments) page and begin the creation of a tournament by clicking on "Create Tournament"

![](1.png)

>This will take you to the Host a New Tournament page where you can select the correct organization (typically A&H Games Monroe or A&H Games Ruston), the tournament location (also typically A&H Games Monroe or A&H Games Ruston) and the game (in this case, Disney Lorcana)
> ![](data:image/png;base64,)

>Next, you will need to fill out some additional information about the event. There are a lot of options here, but the essential ones are as follows. Click **Create Tournament** when finished. 
>|----------|----------|----------|
| **Tournament Name**       | Any name that makes sense for the event |
| **Tournament Type**       | Choose **Tabletop**                     |
| **Tournament Start Date** | Date and time of the event              |
| **Registration Type**     | Choose **Open Registration**            |
| **Entry Type**            | Choose **Free**                         |

>Once you have created the tournamnet you will be taken to the **Tournament Controller** page. The tournament is currently not published, and requires phases to be created first. To do this click on **Phases** on the top of the navigation pane. From there, you can select the **Phase Actions** button and click on **Create a new phase**
>![](data:image/png;base64,)

>On the **Save a Phase of the Tournament** window you can choose  **Free-form**, **Swiss**, **Best of three** and an appropriate number of rounds (this is typically 3 rounds for 8 players) and the click save.
>![](9.png)

> You may now publish the tournament by clicking on **Tournament Actions** and then clicking **Publish tournament**
>![](data:image/png;base64,)

# 🥘 Support Material Acquisition

# 🤾Running Tournament
> First, navigate to the tournament you want to run in melee.gg you can quickly do this by clicking here [My Tournaments](https://melee.gg/Profile/Tournaments) and then clicking on the tournament you wish to run and then clicking on **Tournament Controller**

>On the **Tournament Controller** page you may need to add players if they did not self register. Do this by navigating to **Players**, clicking on **Player Actions**, then **Enroll players**
>![](data:image/png;base64,)

> On the **Enroll a player into Test Tournament** window, you can search for players using their personal details. Once found, click on **Move Details** an then **Enroll exisiting user**. Alterativly you can create an account and enroll if theplayer has never used Melee before.

> Once all players are registered you can start the tournament. Do this by clicking on **Tournament Actions** and then **Start Tournament**
> 
> ![](16.png)> 

> Next, you will need to create the first round of pairings. Do this by navigating to **Matches**, **Match Actions**, and **Match players/teams**
> ![](data:image/png;base64,)

>Players can submit results themselves, but if they are reporting to the store directly, they can be entered from the **Matches** page by clicking on the gear icon next to the pairing and selecting **Edit Result**
>![](data:image/png;base64,)

>The **Edit Result** window looks like this:
>![](data:image/png;base64,)

>Creating the pairings and entering the results will be repeated for each round int the event. Once the last round is completed, results can be viewed by clicking on **Standings** in the **Tournament Controller**
>![](data:image/png;base64,)
